name: Long Island Credit Repair - Y2K Credit Solutions
description_short: Long Island credit repair firm offering dispute-based credit restoration, in-house debt settlement, and tri-merge bureau analysis since 2006.
description_long:
Y2K Credit Solutions was founded in 2006 by Andy Sukhu, a consumer credit specialist with over 20 years of experience, starting out of the family home in Hollis, Queens. The company has since grown to a professional office at 585 Stewart Ave in Garden City, NY (Long Island) and also operates under the name Y2K Debt Solutions. Sukhu, who has appeared on the Earn Your Leisure podcast, built the firm around hands-on, in-person client service — a model that distinguishes it from the wave of mail-order and app-based credit repair operations. The company holds no verified NFCC, HUD, or CDFI certification and is not a nonprofit counseling agency.

The firm's core offering is credit repair through dispute and remediation across all three major credit bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — with a tri-merge credit report pull standard at intake. Named service tiers include Y2K Credit Refresh and Y2K Lite, though specific pricing for either plan is not publicly disclosed. Beyond credit repair, Y2K runs a fully in-house debt settlement department (no third-party outsourcing), and also provides debt consolidation, consumer credit counseling, and financial education. Their navigation includes a business funding section, and they integrate ScoreNavigator as a credit monitoring tool. Negative items they actively address include charge-offs, foreclosures, tax liens, judgments, collections, bankruptcies, repossessions, and identity theft conflicts.

What sets Y2K apart in the crowded Long Island credit repair market is the combination of in-person access and full-service scope. On-site credit analysts work directly with clients rather than routing them through call centers, and the firm claims it can project a client's ending credit score at the very first consultation. They guarantee a score increase within 30 days of starting and assert that removed negative items will not reappear within 90 days. Their review record is notably strong: 4.6 out of 5 stars across 188 Google reviews and 4.5 stars from 169 Birdeye reviews — both well above industry norms for credit repair firms.

For consumers weighing their options, Y2K's strengths are real: 20 years in business, a credible founder with public media presence, in-person consultations, and a one-stop model for repair plus debt settlement without outsourcing. The honest limitations: pricing is entirely opaque until you call or schedule a consult, the company is not BBB accredited and its BBB letter grade is unverified, there is no mobile app or self-serve client portal, and non-profit or regulatory certifications (NFCC, HUD-approved) are absent. Consumers seeking a certified nonprofit counselor or transparent upfront pricing will need to look elsewhere.

pricing.guarantee_details: Claims credit score increase within 30 days of starting service. Asserts removed negative items do not return within 90 days: 'Our work is guaranteed.' No explicit refund policy stated on website.

pros: Founded in 2006 — nearly 20 years of continuous operation as a Long Island credit repair firm
Founder Andy Sukhu has 20+ years as a consumer credit specialist and has appeared on the Earn Your Leisure podcast
Tri-merge credit pull from all three bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion) is standard at intake
In-house debt settlement department handles negotiations directly — no third-party outsourcing
Physical office with on-site credit analysts for in-person consultations, not a call-center model
Claims to project a client's ending credit score at the initial consultation — unusual level of upfront specificity
Strong review profile: 4.6/5 on Google (188 reviews) and 4.5/5 on Birdeye (169 reviews)
cons: No pricing published publicly — costs for Y2K Credit Refresh, Y2K Lite, or debt settlement are unknown until you book a consultation
Not BBB accredited; BBB letter grade unverified and not accessible without direct site access
No mobile app or self-serve online client portal — service is primarily in-person and phone-based
No NFCC, HUD-approved, or CDFI certification — not a nonprofit agency, which matters for consumers seeking free or low-cost regulated counseling
Branch offices in Miami and Chicago reported in search data but not verified on the company's own website
best_for: Long Island and greater NYC metro consumers who want in-person credit repair from an experienced specialist rather than an online-only service
People carrying both damaged credit and unresolved debt who want repair and settlement handled under one roof without being handed off to a third party
Consumers preparing for a mortgage, apartment rental, or auto loan who need a specific score target hit within a defined timeframe
Individuals with complex negative items — foreclosures, tax liens, judgments, or identity theft conflicts — that require more than simple dispute letters
diagnosis: Y2K Credit Solutions is genuinely well-suited for Long Island consumers who want hands-on, in-person credit repair from a firm with a long local track record and the ability to also settle outstanding debt in-house. Andy Sukhu's 20+ years of experience and the company's strong review volume (188 Google, 169 Birdeye) suggest consistent delivery. The primary caveat is cost opacity — pricing is never disclosed publicly, making it impossible to comparison-shop without booking a call — and the absence of any nonprofit or HUD certification means this is a for-profit repair firm, not a free counseling resource.
services: Credit repair and negative item disputes
Tri-merge credit report analysis (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Identity theft and personal ID conflict resolution on credit reports
Tax lien and IRS audit credit impact remediation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 585 Stewart Ave Suite 645, Garden City, NY 11530
